section_path: High Risk Status > HR4: Annex III high-risk use cases
content:
## Decision question: Does your AI system fall within any of the high-risk use case categories listed in Annex III?

Annex III lists stand-alone AI use cases that the EU AI Act treats as high-risk independently of product harmonisation legislation. Unlike the Annex I route, Annex III classification is based on the function and context of the AI system rather than on the product it is embedded in. Under Article 6 point 2, an AI system falling within an Annex III category is presumptively high-risk, subject to the significant risk test in Article 6 point 3.

The Annex III categories are:

- Biometrics
- Critical infrastructure
- Education and vocational training
- Employment, workers management, and access to self-employment
- Access to and enjoyment of essential private services and essential public services and benefits
- Law enforcement
- Migration, asylum, and border control management
- Administration of justice and democratic processes

Detailed definitions and scoping for each category are set out in Annex III itself; organisations should consult the Annex directly when the fit between their system and a category is not obvious.

If the AI system falls within any Annex III category, the assessment proceeds to the significant risk of harm test (HR5), which applies the Article 6 point 3 carve-outs that can rebut the presumption of high-risk classification.

If the AI system does not fall within any Annex III category, it is not high-risk under either the Annex I or Annex III route, and the assessment proceeds to the scope question (S1) without any high-risk classification attached.

Source: Article 6 point 2, Annex III
source_document: FLI EU AI Act Compliance Checker
